original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded Loop in AutoVotingEscrowManager getTotalVotingPower
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `getTotalVotingPower` function iterates over all AVM instances, and each instance iterates over all its locks (up to 1024). This nested loop will quickly exceed the block gas limit, causing the function to revert.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In `AutoVotingEscrowManager.sol`, `getTotalVotingPower` loops over `avms.length`. Inside `AutoVotingEscrow.sol`, `getTotalVotingPower` loops over `locks.length` (max 1024) and calls `balanceOfNFT`, which performs a binary search. With just 2-3 full AVMs, the gas required will exceed the Avalanche block gas limit (8M-15M).

proof_of_signal.pattern: Nested unbounded loops with heavy internal calls

preconditions: Multiple AVM instances are created and filled with locks

initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No pagination or cached total tracking

analysis_questions: How much gas does one balanceOfNFT call consume?
Is getTotalVotingPower used by any critical on-chain logic?

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: An attacker creates many small locks and enables auto-voting, filling up several AVM instances. Any external contract or UI attempting to read `getTotalVotingPower` receives an Out of Gas revert, breaking integrations.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Denial of service for external integrations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires contract upgrade to implement cached tracking

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing gas profiling for full AVMs

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: Calculate max safe locks before OOG

validation_result.mechanism: Unbounded nested loop in view function
validation_result.impact_summary: getTotalVotingPower iterates over all AVMs (unbounded) and all locks per AVM (up to 1024), each making a cross-contract balanceOfNFT call with binary search. Even 1-2 full AVMs will exceed block gas limits, making the function permanently unusable.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies a DoS vulnerability in AutoVotingEscrowManager.getTotalVotingPower(). The function iterates over the avms[] array (unbounded length) and for each AVM calls getTotalVotingPower() on AutoVotingEscrow, which iterates over its locks[] array (up to MAX_LOCKS=1024) making an external call to VotingEscrow.balanceOfNFT() for each lock. balanceOfNFT() performs a binary search through user_point_history, involving multiple storage reads. With even a single full AVM (1024 locks), the gas consumption would be approximately: 1024 × (2600 base call + ~5000 storage reads + binary search overhead) ≈ 8-12M gas, which approaches or exceeds the Avalanche C-Chain block gas limit configured at 8M gas (as shown in hardhat.config.js). With 2+ full AVMs, the function is guaranteed to revert.

The precondition (multiple AVM instances filled with locks) is achievable because enableAutoVoting only requires minBalanceForAutovoting (10 * 1e18 BLACK tokens) per lock and the MAX_LOCKS is 1024. An attacker creating 1024 locks at minimum balance fills one AVM, and the system auto-creates new AVMs when existing ones are full.

While getTotalVotingPower is a view function and doesn't directly block state-changing transactions, it breaks any on-chain or off-chain integration relying on this data. The IAutoVotingEscrowManager interface exposes it, and the IAutomatedVotingManager interface also includes it, meaning any contract calling this will revert.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal at AutoVotingEscrowManager.sol lines 131-135 is accurate. The outer loop over avms[] is clearly unbounded. The inner loop in AutoVotingEscrow.getTotalVotingPower() iterates over all locks (up to 1024) with an external call to balanceOfNFT per iteration. No pagination, caching, or gas-bounding mechanism exists.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Outer loop iterates over unbounded avms[] array. Each iteration makes a cross-contract call to AutoVotingEscrow.getTotalVotingPower() which has its own loop over up to 1024 locks.
Inner loop iterates up to MAX_LOCKS (1024) times, each iteration making an external call to VotingEscrow.balanceOfNFT() which involves storage reads and binary search through user point history.
Each balanceOfNFT call performs a binary search (getPastUserPointIndex) through user_point_history involving multiple SLOAD operations, making each inner loop iteration gas-expensive.

calling_sequence: 2. Function enters for loop: for(uint i=0; i<avms.length; i++)
3. For each AVM, calls avms[i].getTotalVotingPower() - cross-contract call
4. AutoVotingEscrow.getTotalVotingPower() enters inner loop: for(uint i=0; i<locks.length; i++)
5. For each lock, calls IVotingEscrow(votingEscrow).balanceOfNFT(locks[i].tokenId) - another cross-contract call
7. Library performs binary search via getPastUserPointIndex() with multiple storage reads
8. Returns voting power for single NFT, loop continues to next lock
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The O(N*M) complexity where N=avms.length (unbounded) and M=locks.length (up to 1024), combined with the gas cost per iteration (cross-contract call + binary search + storage reads ≈ 8,000-15,000 gas per lock), means that gas consumption grows as: N * M * ~10,000 gas. With N=1 and M=1024, this is ~10M gas already exceeding the 8M gas limit in hardhat.config.js (gas: 8000000). New AVMs are automatically created when existing ones fill up (enableAutoVoting creates new AutoVotingEscrow when all existing ones are full), so N grows naturally with user adoption.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: hardhat.config.js shows gas: 8000000 for avalanche network, confirming the 8M gas limit. The WEEK constant is 1800 seconds, meaning epochs are short and more users may use AVM over time.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: An attacker creates 1024+ veNFT locks with minimum 10 BLACK tokens each (10,240 BLACK total for one full AVM) and enables auto-voting for all of them. The first 1024 fill one AVM, additional locks create new AVMs. Once 1+ AVMs are full, getTotalVotingPower() reverts with out-of-gas. The attack cost is 10,240+ BLACK tokens (which remain locked and retain value as veNFTs, so the attacker doesn't lose their principal). Any protocol integration, governance contract, or frontend calling getTotalVotingPower() permanently fails. Even without a deliberate attack, normal organic growth of AVM usage will eventually trigger this issue.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM severity. The affected function is view-only, so no state-changing operations are directly blocked. However: (1) Any on-chain contract that calls getTotalVotingPower() will revert, breaking composability. (2) Off-chain UIs and indexers relying on this function will fail. (3) The issue becomes worse over time as more users adopt auto-voting. (4) No mitigation exists without contract upgrade since there's no pagination parameter.
recommended_mitigations: Add pagination to getTotalVotingPower: function getTotalVotingPower(uint256 avmStart, uint256 avmEnd) with bounded range
Implement a cached total voting power variable that is updated incrementally when locks are added/removed/voted
Add a getTotalVotingPowerForAVM(uint256 avmIndex, uint256 lockStart, uint256 lockEnd) function for paginated per-AVM queries
Consider reducing MAX_LOCKS from 1024 to a lower value (e.g., 256) to reduce per-AVM gas consumption
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy VotingEscrow with BLACK token. 2. Deploy AutoVotingEscrowManager. 3. Create 1024 veNFT locks with minimum balance. 4. Enable auto-voting for all 1024 to fill one AVM. 5. Call getTotalVotingPower() and measure gas - expect ~8-12M gas. 6. Create another 1024 locks and fill a second AVM. 7. Call getTotalVotingPower() again - expect revert with OOG.
uncertainties: Exact gas per balanceOfNFT call depends on number of user point epochs (binary search depth)
Whether any on-chain contract currently calls getTotalVotingPower() - no such call found in provided code, reducing practical on-chain impact
Actual block gas limit on production Avalanche deployment may differ from hardhat config
additional_testing_needed: Gas profiling with 256, 512, 1024 locks in a single AVM to find exact threshold
Check if any deployed contracts (not in scope) call getTotalVotingPower()
Test with multiple AVMs to confirm linear gas growth
